geography.data_source: MCGM ward boundaries (L-ward mapped to numeric IDs); area from MCGM Development Plan 2034

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 L-ward Mumbai suburban district; very high density zone; household size from NSSO 76th Round Mumbai suburban lower-mid-income estimates; literacy from Census 2011 L-ward; SC/ST from Census 2011

income_profile.data_source: Median from PLFS 2022-23 Mumbai suburban lower-mid income quintile; distribution left-skewed reflecting lower-mid income character; informal rate from NSSO 78th Round for L-ward area; occupations reflect Kurla's transit hub economy, BKC proximity service jobs, and local garment/manufacturing

commute_profile.data_source: Kurla is a major interchange — Central Railway + Harbour Line + Bus terminus; Metro Line 1 terminates at nearby Ghatkopar; MCTS 2016 mode share for L-ward TAZs; low car ownership reflecting lower-mid income; auto-rickshaw dominant last-mile

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: High fare sensitivity from lower-mid income + transit dependency; low collective action threshold from historically high political mobilisation in Kurla; low railway trust from chronic overcrowding and safety concerns at Kurla station; Lokniti-CSDS 2024 Mumbai suburban sub-sample; political data from Kurla Assembly constituency 2024

social_network_parameters.data_source: High clustering from dense residential + religious community networks; high connections from transit hub bringing diverse populations through; low cross-income bridging reflecting income homogeneity; high tier2 density from active political mobilisation

informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Kurla station is a major interchange; vendors on both east and west sides; footfall from Central Railway + Harbour Line combined Kurla station data
Nehru Nagar commercial area; MCGM trade license data for L-ward
Informal service economy — Kurla residents providing cleaning, security, catering, and transport services to BKC offices; estimated from BKC facility management employment data
LBS Road commercial stretch through Kurla; MCGM and BEST route ridership data
Vendors at Kurla bus terminus (one of BEST's busiest); footfall from BEST terminus ridership data
Small-scale garment manufacturing and tailoring units; MCGM industrial license records for L-ward

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: Transit Hub/Mixed zone template from AGENT_SPEC.md; Kurla is a major transit interchange with dense mixed-income population; sum = 1.00

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Tier 2 counts from MCGM corporator data for L-ward, community organisation records, religious institution heads reflecting Kurla's diverse religious demography
